items.title: Feature extraction builds on vectorized arrays
items.description: Feature extraction produces numeric representations — bag-of-words counts, TF-IDF vectors, image flattening — that land in NumPy ndarrays. Vectorization is how you scale, clip, and combine those features efficiently before modeling.

items.title: Design matrices live in vector spaces
items.description: Each row of a NumPy design matrix is a feature vector in R^n. Vectorized column operations (centering, scaling, projecting) are the computational form of linear operations on those vectors.

items.title: Gradient descent needs fast array math
items.description: Training loops compute gradients and parameter updates on large weight and activation arrays. The same vectorization mindset — whole-array ops, broadcasting, contiguous memory — keeps optimization steps fast on CPU or GPU backends.

items.title: Pandas groupby often delegates to NumPy
items.description: Pandas is ideal for labeled tabular transforms and joins; once you need raw speed on numeric blocks, `.values` or `.to_numpy()` hands work to NumPy vectorization. Knowing both tools lets you pick the right layer of the pipeline.
